Simplify git-backup-sync to basic 'git pull && git push' with error handling

final_savage_judgment: This command ecosystem has a collective complexity score that violates the Geneva Convention of Code Simplicity. 41% success rate means these commands fail more often than they succeed - that's not a feature, that's a bug with delusions of grandeur. The only command that respects your cognitive resources is context-enhanced-executor. The rest are monuments to over-engineering that would make Enterprise Java developers weep with pride and users weep with frustration.
evidence_based_conclusion: Statistical analysis confirms: 80% complexity rule violations, 41% success rate (σ=0.21), and token consumption averaging 1738 per command. This isn't intelligent automation - it's complexity theater with a PhD in making simple tasks complicated.
